title: IPL: Shreyas Iyer’s maiden IPL ton keeps PBKS playoff hopes alive with big win over LSG
content: Punjab Kings ended a six-match losing streak with a dominant seven-wicket victory over Lucknow Super Giants. Captain Shreyas Iyer smashed an unbeaten century, supported by Prabhsimran Singh's quickfire 69. This win propels Punjab to fourth place, but their playoff qualification still depends on other match outcomes. Lucknow had earlier posted 196/6 thanks to Josh Inglis and Ayush Badoni.

title: CEO Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: Google co-founder Sergey Brin donates $500,000 to California group, and this is why
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is injecting significant funds into San Francisco's local politics, backing a business-friendly tax proposal while opposing a controversial "Overpaid CEO Tax."  This move, following his relocation from Silicon Valley, reflects Brin's broader campaign against wealth redistribution measures, which he links to his personal experience fleeing socialism.

title: Odisha fugitive missing for 32 years found in cellar hideout
content: A 32-year manhunt concluded with the arrest of Rabindra Mallick, accused of a 1994 murder. Police discovered him in a secret underground chamber at his Jajpur home after he returned for a family event. Mallick had lived under false identities and worked as a mason in various states, evading capture for decades. His bail plea was denied.

title: Tamil Nadu minor girl murder: CM Vijay orders fast-track probe, vows strict punishment
content: Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay has ordered a swift and intensive investigation into the abduction and murder of a 10-year-old girl in Coimbatore. Assuring severe punishment for those involved in crimes against women and children, he stated that two individuals have been arrested and a speedy probe is underway to file the chargesheet immediately.

title: Full breakdown of USCIS memo on change in Green Card rules for H1-B, L-1, F-1 OPT
content: USCIS has issued a new policy memo requiring most foreigners seeking a green card to apply from their home country, shifting from adjustment of status within the U.S. This stricter standard emphasizes weighing negative factors like immigration violations and inconsistent conduct, demanding stronger evidence of "unusual or outstanding" positive ties to the U.S. for those applying domestically.

title: Donald Trump opposes Jaxson Dart playing against women: Report
content: During a campaign event, US President Donald Trump unexpectedly praised New York Giants quarterback Jaxson Dart’s physique. He reportedly linked it to his stance against transgender athletes in women’s sports. Trump questioned if any woman could tackle Dart. He highlighted Dart’s muscular build. The comments drew mixed reactions online, with some criticizing the focus on physical appearance over politics.

title: Kylian Mbappe’s Real Madrid future in focus amid Chelsea rumors
content: Kylian Mbappe has been linked with a surprise move to Chelsea after another frustrating season at Real Madrid despite his excellent goal record. Football expert Andy Brassell believes Mbappe’s strong connection with Xabi Alonso could play a key role in the future. Chelsea were interested in Mbappe during his youth years, and fresh transfer speculation has now returned again.

title: Toronto Maple Leafs face uncertainty amid Matthew Knies trade rumors
content: The Toronto Maple Leafs have several offseason questions to answer, and the trade talk surrounding forward Matthew Knies is heating up. Knies is still young and promising, but there is speculation that he might be used in a major roster move. Meanwhile, San Jose Sharks defenceman Mario Ferraro has emerged as a possible defensive target, adding to the complexity of Toronto’s NHL offseason plans.

title: US, Iran move closer to 60-day ceasefire extension: What's in the deal
content: The US and Iran are reportedly nearing a 60-day ceasefire extension, with mediators working on an interim deal. This agreement could pave the way for broader talks on Iran's nuclear program and regional security, including the gradual reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and phased sanctions relief for Tehran.

title: Arjun Tendulkar's joy turns to frustration after Rishabh Pant spills chance - WATCH
content: Arjun Tendulkar made his IPL debut for Lucknow Super Giants against Punjab Kings, bowling his first over immediately after the Powerplay and impressing with his line and length. He also remained unbeaten on five runs as LSG posted 196/6. Josh Inglis and Ayush Badoni were key contributors with the bat for LSG.

title: Swipe, scroll, repeat: How algorithms are shaping young minds
content: Social media's pervasive influence is reshaping childhood, impacting attention spans, emotional regulation, and self-esteem among Indian youth. Experts and parents highlight concerns over digital addiction, cyberbullying, and the erosion of real-world interactions. While acknowledging its benefits, the article stresses the need for balanced, mindful usage and open communication to navigate this digital landscape.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for May 24, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for May 24, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Arshdeep Singh scripts unwanted IPL record in nightmare spell vs LSG
content: Arshdeep Singh endured a costly spell, conceding 52 runs in three overs, setting an unwanted PBKS record. Lucknow Super Giants posted 196, driven by Josh Inglis's aggressive 72 and Ayush Badoni's quickfire 43. Despite early setbacks, LSG's strong batting performance, including contributions from Rishabh Pant and Abdul Samad, secured a competitive total.

title: Is United States on verge of a Maduro-like military intervention in Cuba?
content: The US under Trump is intensifying pressure on Cuba, drawing comparisons to its Venezuela policy. However, experts believe Cuba's complex political structure and disciplined security apparatus make it a far riskier and potentially costlier challenge for Washington than Venezuela.

title: Twisha Sharma case timeline: Marriage, mysterious death, FIR and courtroom battle
content: A model-turned-actor's death in Bhopal has ignited a legal storm. Her family alleges dowry harassment and abetment to suicide against her husband and his family, including a retired judge. Investigations are intensifying with an SIT formed, bail rejections, and a potential CBI probe now on the horizon.

title: Huge blow for RCB! Star player leaves IPL 2026 camp just before playoffs
content: Jacob Bethell's IPL 2026 campaign with Royal Challengers Bengaluru has been cut short due to a finger injury, raising concerns for England's upcoming Test series against New Zealand. The young batter, who impressed in the Ashes, will return home for assessment, leaving his availability for the Lord's Test uncertain. England's medical team will monitor his recovery.

title: Hantavirus panic: Are we reliving Covid fears all over again?
content: Public anxiety over hantavirus is amplified by COVID-19's lingering trauma, leading to comparisons of potential pandemics. However, experts clarify that hantavirus, primarily spread through rodent contact, lacks the efficient human-to-human transmission that fueled COVID-19's global spread, making widespread outbreaks unlikely.

title: 'I'd say sorry': Why Rishabh Pant apologised in LSG's last game
content: Lucknow Super Giants captain Rishabh Pant apologized to fans for a disappointing IPL 2026 season, as his team, already out of playoff contention, faced Punjab Kings.  Pant acknowledged the tough campaign, promising a 100% effort in their final league match. Punjab Kings, needing a win to stay alive, opted to bowl first.

title: Raveena Tandon urges Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann to keep stray dog drive humane
content: Raveena Tandon has urged Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann to handle the state's stray dog management with compassion, following a Supreme Court order. While acknowledging public safety concerns, Tandon advocated for humane solutions like sterilization and shelters, suggesting Punjab could lead by example in balancing animal welfare with citizen security.

title: Watch: Pentagon releases footage of US jet shooting down mysterious UFO
content: Pentagon has released a new video showing a US fighter jet downing an unidentified object over Lake Huron in February 2023. This infrared footage, part of a declassified batch, depicts a "kinetic interaction" leading to the object's fragmentation. The release includes hundreds of files, documents, and videos related to "unidentified anomalous phenomena" (UAP), fueling public curiosity.

title: Not brain drain but brain circulation: Why 70% of Indian graduates are heading abroad
content: Indian graduates are increasingly pursuing higher education abroad, with nearly 70% planning to study overseas for better exposure and skills. However, most intend to return to India for employment, reflecting growing confidence in the domestic job market. The trend signals a shift from traditional brain drain to “brain circulation” driven by global learning and local opportunities.

title: West Bengal gained infamy due to recruitment scams, new recruitment policy in next Budget session: CM
content: The allegation of corruption in government jobs came to hurt the Trinamool Congress and the party lost the recently concluded assembly elections to the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP).

title: Why did Kerala scrap the SilverLine project?
content: What was the project and why was it projected as a game-changer for Kerala’s transport sector? Why did the land acquisition process trigger protests? What processes were put in place to acquire land? What were the concerns over the project?

title: What did the Supreme Court say about bail under UAPA?
content: What are the disagreements between recent Supreme Court rulings on UAPA bail? Why is bail so difficult under Section 43D(5) of the UAPA? What did the Court say in K.A. Najeeb about prolonged incarceration and bail? What role does Article 21 play in UAPA bail jurisprudence?

title: ₹350 crore investment to generate 2,300 employment opportunities in Bihar
content: Industries Minister Shreyasi Singh said that the newly approved industrial projects are set to provide fresh momentum to Bihar’s industrial and technological development

title: How has the BCCI resisted RTI scrutiny?
content: Why does the body not qualify as a public authority? What does the CIC order say? What are the implications?

title: Frequent disruption of ro-ro services give harrowing time to commuters
content: Owing to the delay in inducting the third ro-ro ferry, demand is rife that the Kochi Corporation impose curbs on transporting goods carriers on the Fort Kochi-Vypeen stretch during peak hours

title: DMK, Congress trade barbs over the latter’s post-poll alliance with the TVK
content: Speaking at a DMK youth wing meeting, Leader of the Opposition Udhayanidhi Stalin slams the Congress and says the national party is responsible for the BJP’s continuous victories in the country and alleges that the DMK has carried such a party on its shoulders in the Lok Sabha and recent Assembly elections

title: TVK government will fall one day, says M.K. Stalin
content: Addressing the party’s youth wing meeting, he asserts that what has happened in T.N. is “not a political tsunami, but a cinema tsunami” and likenes public support for Vijay to a child’s fascination with a new doll

title: Twisha Sharma death case: husband Samarth Singh brought to Bhopal, police to seek remand
content: Bhopal Commissioner said local police would continue the investigation until the central agency formally takes over the case; yet to be reached for recording my statement, says Giribala Singh

title: Power cuts caused by load issues, not shortage, says Minister R. Nirmalkumar
content: He says the Electricity Department has constituted seven rapid response teams of engineers in Chennai, and they have been provided vehicles to swiftly reach spots and rectify complaints

title: Petrol, diesel prices hiked again in Bengaluru; transport sector warns of ripple effect
content: Oil companies had earlier revised prices upward by around ₹3 per litre on May 15, followed by another 90 paise increase on May 19

title: I don’t think the ruling party has the stomach for sarcasm, says Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke
content: Abhijeet Dipke, founder of the ‘Cockroach Janta Party’, alleges threats and censorship following the takedown of his youth-led satirical political outfit’s website

title: Fuel price hike: Bengaluru hoteliers feel the heat
content: Almost everything restaurants depend on- vegetables, milk, meat, groceries, cooking supplies, and even laundry materials- arrives through transport networks dependent on diesel.

title: Speakers at leadership summit emphasise on sustained deliberation on role of AI in critical sectors
content: Pro Chancellor of KLE Technological University Prof. Ashok Shettar has emphasised the need for a sustained deliberation on the role of Artificial Intelligence and its implications across critical sectors such as education, banking, defence, and healthcare

title: Hubballi Dharwad Central MLA  tells officials to facilitate export of mangoes grown in region
content: Emphasising the need for facilitating export of the mangoes grown by the farmers of the region, Hubballi Dharwad Central MLA Mahesh Tenginakai has said the officials should provide requisite information and handhold the farmers initially

title: Centre committed to ensuring transparent, corruption-free recruitmentm says Union Minister Joshi
content: Union Minister for Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution Pralhad Joshi has said that the union government was committed to ensuring transparent and corruption-free recruitment processes.

title: KSRTC driver suspended over defamatory post against Kerala Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan
content: Transport corporation initiates departmental disciplinary action against the driver attached to the KSRTC Nedumkandam unit in Idukki

title: Karnataka to soon get India’s second largest international cricket stadium
content: The move comes despite opposition from a section of farmers and environmentalists who say the proposed stadium, which will come up close to the Karadikkal-Mahadeshwara wildlife corridor, will have an adverse impact on the environment.

title: Supreme Court registers suo motu case over death of Twisha Sharma
content: A three-judge Bench comprising Chief Justice of India Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipul M. Pancholi is scheduled to hear the matter on Monday (May 25)

title: CPI(M) demands scrapping NTA, seeks Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation
content: The party claimed that since the NTA was set up in 2017, "there have been at least four instances when leakages were exposed" and alleged that the agency had been plagued by multiple problems while conducting examinations

title: Marco Rubio in India highlights: PM Modi, Rubio discuss bilateral ties, West Asia crisis
content: Rubio invites PM Modi to White House; they discussed India-U.S. Comprehensive Global Strategic Partnership and issues related to regional and global peace and security

title: Agri-tech has improved productivity and post-harvest management, say experts at webinar
content: An expert said that 47% of farm work was now mechanised, and that the introduction of scientific methods has led to surplus production

title: Cabinet clears 2.5 lakh houses, ₹587 crore for PRLSI
content: State to join RDSS and ₹300 crore sanctioned as first instalment for preparations for Godavari Pushkarams, mid-day meals in junior colleges

title: Twisha Sharma’s husband sent to 7-day police remand; AIIMS Delhi forms panel for second autopsy
content: Police told The Hindu that during the remand period, the SIT probing the case will recreate the scene of crime along with seizing substantial evidence from him and interrogating him on various aspects

title: MHA warns of phishing campaign targeting users of lost or stolen iPhones
content: Hackers impersonate Apple Support and exploit victims’ urgency to locate or secure their missing devices through fraudulent SMS messages containing phishing links

title: U.P. Congress chief Ajay Rai booked over ‘objectionable’ comment on PM Modi
content: Mr. Rai hit back calling the FIR an intimidation tactic and accusing the BJP government of misusing the State apparatus and police

title: Uppal flyover works push traffic into residential lanes in eastern Hyderabad, double commute times for residents
content: Diversions through colony roads hit local businesses, parking and daily commute at Uppal X roads and surrounding areas

title: Nursing student dies during surgery at Tiruchi government hospital; colleagues stage protest
content: A high-level inquiry committee, comprising doctors of the Madras Medical College and Rajiv Gandhi Government General Hospital (RGGGH), has been constituted to inquire into the incident

title: A.P. continues to grapple with intense heat as 95 mandals record above 42°C
content: Kanumolu in Krishna district records 45.8°C; severe heatwave conditions may prevail in 21 mandals while heatwave conditions may affect 214 mandals on Sunday, says APSDMA

title: Manipur CM assures NIA probe into abduction of Naga men
content: Kuki Inpi Manipur extends shutdown since May 13 by another 48 hours to secure release of 14 Kuki people

title: Telangana proceeds with plan to relocate polluting units outside city, issues HILTP norms
content: Minister for Industries and IT D. Sridhar Babu launched a dedicated online portal for receiving applications from eligible industrial units seeking to avail benefits under the policy

title: States to get VB-G RAM G funding based on 16th Finance Commission formula, ‘performance criteria’
content: Draft rules open for feedback till June 21; 16-member National Level Steering Committee for the new scheme must include five State representatives; rules also spell out provisions for the transition from MGNREGA

title: BMCRI working on setting up a centralised diagnostic hub to ease burden on Victoria Hospital’s Infosys central laboratory
content: A sharp rise in patient load is resulting in long delays for tests and reports at the central diagnostic laboratory built by the Infosys Foundation

title: Congress slams Centre over rising fuel prices, questions PM for not disclosing seriousness of crisis
content: Congress president accuses Centre of ‘looting the public in instalments’, says benefits were not passed down to people when global prices were low

title: Experts urge hydration, awareness to prevent heat-related illnesses
content: Prolonged exposure to high temperatures can affect the body’s natural thermoregulation mechanisms and lead to a range of health conditions, experts said at a webinar organised by The Hindu on Saturday

title: India is important to the U.S.’s Indo-Pacific policy, says Rubio, invites Modi to Washington
content: The U.S. Secretary of State is in India for a four-day visit to hold bilateral talks, and attend the Quad Foreign Ministers’ Meeting

title: On Trinamool Turncoats In Bengal, BJP Between A Rock And A Hard Place
content: The BJP's state unit has bluntly declined to give way to prospective Trinamool turncoats, arguing the party isn't a dharmshala.

title: Refrain From Cow Slaughter: Assam Eid Committees Ahead Of Bakri Eid
content: The appeal from the committees in Assam, which has 35 per cent Muslim population, came following an appeal by Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma on Saturday.

title: Jaipur Woman Dies By Suicide After Domestic Abuse, Children Recount Torture
content: Days after the incident, Mahir accessed his mother's phone and retrieved several videos, including footage of an altercation.

title: Dharmendra Pradhan Seeks CBSE Report Over Re-Evaluation Glitches
content: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan has sought a detailed report from the CBSE over complaints of technical glitches faced by students and parents during the re-evaluation process, a source said on Saturday.

title: After PM's 'Satisfactory' Appraisal Rating, Ministers Swing Into Action
content: The chief yardstick for the performance appraisal was the ministries' agility in complaint redressal and file disposal.

title: 'US Not Honest, Iran Won't Compromise In Talks': Top Negotiator To Pak
content: Iran's top negotiator Mohammad Baqer Ghalibaf told Pakistani army chief Asim Munir during talks in Tehran that the US was not an honest party in negotiations.

title: Trump Says Iran Deal Has "Solid 50/50" Chance, Warns Of Strikes If Talks Fail
content: Trump also warned that if the US and Iran do not come to an agreement, "we're going to have a situation where no country will ever be hit as hard as they're about to be hit."

title: NIA To Take Up Cases Of 3 Thadou Leaders Killing, 6 Missing Nagas In Manipur
content: Search and rescue operations have been launched since May 14 to trace the whereabouts of the six Naga villagers, Manipur Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh said

title: India Eyes $50 Billion Trade Goal As Piyush Goyal Set For Canada Visit
content: Goyal will be accompanied by senior executives from more than 100 Indian companies representing sectors such as energy, metals and mining, aerospace, telecom, pharmaceuticals, agriculture, tourism, textiles, automotive, and capital goods.

title: Delhi Heatwave Back After Brief Relief, Weather Office Issues Warning
content: Residents breathed a collective sigh of relief on Saturday as strong winds, thunderstorms, lightning, and scattered rainfall swept Delhi.

title: "Don't Know Whom To Trust": Twisha Sharma's Brother As 2nd Post-Mortem Looms
content: In the deeply sensitive and increasingly explosive Twisha Sharma death case, the next seven days may prove decisive not just for the police investigation but for the family's long battle for answers.

title: Supreme Court Takes Up Twisha Sharma Case, Hearing On May 25
content: Supreme Court takes up Twisha Sharma case, Chief Justice of India Surya Kant to hear matter on May 25

title: Trump To Award Highest Civilian Honour To 9/11 Hero Who Died Saving 18 People
content: On the morning of the attacks, Welles Remy Crowther had called his mother to tell her he was safe after the planes struck. Hours later, his body was recovered from the wreckage of the collapsed South Tower.

title: 'Dragged, Pulled By Hair': Flotilla Activists Recount Torture By Israel
content: Israel denies mistreatment. The allegations were "false and entirely without factual basis," said Zivan Freidin, a spokesperson for the Israeli Prison Service.

title: Efforts To End Iran War On, No Timeline On Strikes: Marco Rubio To NDTV
content: Rubio told NDTV that US President Donald Trump prefers to solve these two problems - the nuclear issue and opening the Strait of Hormuz through a negotiated agreement and diplomacy.

title: Raghav Chadha Appointed Chairman Of Rajya Sabha Committee On Petitions
content: Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha who switched from the AAP to the BJP recently has been appointed as the new chairman of the Committee on Petitions of the Upper House.

title: US Announces "America First" Visa Tool For "Vital" India Ties. What It Means
content: The tool is built around a single prioritisation principle: applicants who strengthen ties with the United States get earlier access to scheduling.

title: Delhi Continues To Sizzles As Mercury Crosses 44-Degrees Celcius Mark
content: Dry scorching winds and relentless heat turned Delhi into a furnace on Monday as the maximum temperatures breached the 44 degrees Celsius mark in parts of the national capital, with the city staring at an extended spell of punishing heat in the upcoming week.

title: Kashmir nomads file ‘atrocity’ complaint after demolition of their houses
content: nomadic Gujjars and backerwals have filed a complaint with the police . they allege the demolitions are in violation of the SC/ST and Forest Rights Act . the demolished structures were protected under the law and no prior notice, eviction order, rehabilitation plan or opportunity of hearing was provided .

title: 5-nation tour pacts to drive job growth: PM Modi
content: pacts signed with the UAE and Norway will strengthen shipbuilding ecosystem . "Demand for engineers, technicians and skilled workers will surge beyond your imagination," he tells young audience . he says opportunities for the youth across manufacturing, technology, startups, digital services, railways, defence are unprecedented in the country's history .

title: ‘Can’t hit kid’s dignity to prove adultery’: HC no to DNA test
content: Sanjeev Verma is a Senior Assistant Editor covering Himachal Pradesh for The Times of India. His journalism experience spans across multiple states, including Punjab and Haryana, in addition to covering the Punjab and Haryana High Court, after an initial stint in Delhi. He navigates the realms of politics, security, public policy, finance, industries and commerce, rural development, legal affairs, environment, defence services welfare, and NRI affairs, with a focus on investigative journalism. title: Jammu-Srinagar Vande Bharat crosses 1 lakh passenger mark in just 22 days
content:
JAMMU: Jammu-Srinagar Vande Bharat Express, the first ever direct train flagged off between the two cities on May 2, achieved the unprecedented milestone of carrying 1.01 lakh passengers within just 22 days. Northern Railway’s senior divisional commercial manager, Uchit Singhal, said, “We have received extremely positive feedback from passengers regarding the train’s comfort, safety and hospitality. This train has put an end to the traditional fatigue of the Jammu-Srinagar route.” The train has operated with excellent punctuality and 100% reliability, said public relations inspector Raghvender Singh. “The travel between Jammu and Srinagar otherwise was dependent on national highway-44, which often faced disruptions due to landslides and inclement weather,” he added.

End of Article

title: From H-1B to 'maybe': Green card dream hits red light
content: a sweeping new directive issued by the US Citizenship and Immigration Services . most temporary visa holders seeking permanent residency will now be required to leave the US and apply for green cards from their home countries . the policy marks one of the most dramatic attempts yet by the Trump administration to curb illegal immigration .

title: NEET paper leak: Arrested headmistress recalled physics questions from memory, says CBI
content: city-based school headmistress manisha Havaldar allegedly reconstructed NEET physics questions from memory and shared them with a student . she was subsequently taken to Delhi for further questioning as part of the ongoing investigation in the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak case .

title: Rubio meets PM Modi, delivers Trump's invite to visit US in ‘near future’
content: the two sides discussed the situation in west Asia, according to a readout . Rubio emphasised that the US will not let Iran hold the global energy market hostage and affirmed that US energy products have the potential to diversify India’s energy supply .

title: ‘Reward for theft?’ Kerala oppn targets UDF as poll officer appointed CM’s secretary
content: both BJP and BJP have come out against the state govt's decision to appoint chief electoral officer Rathan Khelkar as chief minister V D Satheesan's principal secretary . the state secretariat says the appointment is a step that calls into question the credibility of the assembly elections itself . former state president K Surendran was the first to raise the alleged double standard of Congress .

title: Only 6 states now have fertility rate above replacement level
content: india's total fertility rate (TFR), or the average number of children a woman would have, has fallen even further to 1.9 from 2.1, according to the latest sample registration system (SRS) report of 2024 . the TFR has fallen below the replacement level for all other states .

title: US pushes Mauritius down to become 2nd largest source of FDI
content: equity investments from the us more than doubled to over $11 billion in 2025-26 . Singapore remained the top source, registering a healthy increase . japan saw a hefty increase th-anks to large cheques in the financial services space .

title: 'Attempt to malign force’s image': Army on video on a party presser
content: the video of a press conference at the press club of india was circulated on various media and social media platforms . it alleges that it was a malicious propaganda campaign designed to malign the force’s image and push fabricated narratives . the Indian Army clarified that the individuals seen in the viral clip had been dismissed from service on grounds of indiscipline and unsoldierly conduct .

title: Leh chopper crash: Major General poses for selfie after accident, pilot flashes victory sign

title: Green card? H-1Bs must prove worthy of ‘status adjustment’
content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies. The Indian diaspora is the largest in the world; through her articles she demystifies the immigration-policy related developments in select countries for outbound students, job aspirants and employees. She also analyses the impact of Income-tax and GST related developments for individuals and business entities. Read More

title: Strong El Nino may peak this year, weaken before India’s 2027 monsoon
content: Neha Madaan is a senior feature writer at The Times of India, Pune. She holds an M A degree in Mass Communication and Journalism from University of Pune. She covers tourism, heritage development and its conservation, apart from an array of subjects such as civic issues, environment, astronomy, civic school education as well as social issues concerning persons with disabilities. Her interests include metaphysical research and animal rights. Read More

title: Delhi's Gymkhana Club near PM residence told to vacate over national security needs
content: govt had given 27.3 acres of land on perpetual lease to DGC in 1928 . the club is located next to PM’s residence in Lutyens’ Delhi . it has been operated from 2, Safdarjung Road since 1913 .

title: 'Lured foreigners with fat perks': Govt says 217 Indians joined Russian army, 49 dead
content: as many as 217 Indians have joined Russian forces, of which 49 died and six are missing in action in the ongoing Russia-Ukraine war . the govt said the Indian Embassy is making efforts to bring back the 139 people who have been released from military service .
